questions.title: Orchestration or choreography for a booking workflow
questions.prompt: A booking process reserves inventory, authorizes payment, creates a ticket, updates loyalty, and sends notifications. Would you use orchestration or choreography?
questions.answerStructureHint: Separate the critical ordered workflow from independent reactions, then cover state, compensation, and observability.

expectedPoints.label: Identifies the critical ordered steps
expectedPoints.description: Treats inventory, payment, and ticket creation as a workflow whose order and outcome must be explicit.

expectedPoints.label: Justifies orchestration for the core
expectedPoints.description: Uses a coordinator for the bounded critical workflow when progress, compensation, and debugging matter.

expectedPoints.label: Uses choreography for independent reactions
expectedPoints.description: Moves loyalty and notifications to events when they do not gate booking confirmation.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps domain rules with services
expectedPoints.description: Ensures the orchestrator coordinates but payment and inventory services own their rules.

expectedPoints.label: Designs operability
expectedPoints.description: Includes correlation, timeout, retry, terminal states, workflow inspection, and repair.

followUps.prompt: Payment succeeds but ticket creation fails. What state and compensation would you design?

hu.title: Orchestration vagy choreography egy booking workflow-hoz
hu.prompt: Egy booking process inventoryt reserve-ol, paymentet authorize-ol, ticketet hoz létre, loyaltyt frissít és notificationt küld. Orchestrationt vagy choreographyt használnál?
hu.answerStructureHint: Különítsd el a critical ordered workflow-t az independent reactionöktől, majd térj ki state-re, compensationre és observabilityre.

arch_orch_critical.label: Azonosítja a critical ordered stepeket
arch_orch_critical.description: Az inventory, payment és ticket creation explicit orderrel és outcome-mal rendelkező workflow.

arch_orch_choice.label: A core flow-hoz indokolja az orchestrationt
arch_orch_choice.description: Coordinator kezeli a bounded critical workflow progresszét, compensationjét és debuggingját.

arch_orch_events.label: Independent reactionhöz choreographyt használ
arch_orch_events.description: Loyalty és notification eventre kerül, ha nem gate-eli a booking confirmationt.

arch_orch_domain.label: A domain rule a service-ben marad
arch_orch_domain.description: Az orchestrator koordinál, de payment és inventory service ownolja a saját rule-jait.

arch_orch_ops.label: Operabilityt tervez
arch_orch_ops.description: Correlation, timeout, retry, terminal state, workflow inspection és repair szerepel.

followUps.arch_orch_followup_failure: A payment sikerül, de a ticket creation elbukik. Milyen state-et és compensationt terveznél?

questions.title: When CQRS is justified
questions.prompt: A portfolio system has complex renewal rules on writes and several high-volume dashboards and search views. Would CQRS help?
questions.answerStructureHint: Explain the write/read mismatch, propose the simplest CQRS form, and own projection lag and recovery.

expectedPoints.label: Identifies a real read/write mismatch
expectedPoints.description: Shows that complex write invariants and differently shaped high-volume reads can justify separate models.

expectedPoints.label: Separates command and query responsibilities
expectedPoints.description: Keeps authoritative state and rules on the write side and creates query-specific read models.

expectedPoints.label: Owns eventual consistency
expectedPoints.description: Explains projection lag, pending states, freshness expectations, and user-visible consequences.

expectedPoints.label: Makes projections operable
expectedPoints.description: Includes idempotent updates, checkpoints, monitoring, replay, and rebuild.

expectedPoints.label: Avoids unnecessary infrastructure
expectedPoints.description: Notes that CQRS can start as logical separation and does not require event sourcing or two databases.

followUps.prompt: A projection misses events and becomes inconsistent. How do you detect and rebuild it?

hu.title: Mikor indokolt a CQRS?
hu.prompt: Egy portfolio system write oldalán komplex renewal rule-ok vannak, miközben több nagy forgalmú dashboard és search view kell. Segítene a CQRS?
hu.answerStructureHint: Mutasd meg a write/read mismatch-et, javasold a legegyszerűbb CQRS formát, és vállald a projection lagot és recoveryt.

arch_cqrs_fit.label: Valódi read/write mismatch-et azonosít
arch_cqrs_fit.description: A complex write invariant és eltérő shape-ű high-volume read indokolhat separate modelt.

arch_cqrs_models.label: Elkülöníti a command és query responsibilityt
arch_cqrs_models.description: Az authoritative state és rule a write oldalon marad, a queryhez külön read model készül.

arch_cqrs_consistency.label: Vállalja az eventual consistencyt
arch_cqrs_consistency.description: Projection lag, pending state, freshness expectation és user-visible következmény szerepel.

arch_cqrs_recovery.label: Operable projectiont tervez
arch_cqrs_recovery.description: Idempotent update, checkpoint, monitoring, replay és rebuild kerül elő.

arch_cqrs_scope.label: Kerüli a fölösleges infrastructure-t
arch_cqrs_scope.description: A CQRS indulhat logical separationnel, és nem követel event sourcingot vagy két database-t.

followUps.arch_cqrs_followup_missed: Egy projection eventeket veszít és inconsistent lesz. Hogyan detektálnád és rebuildelnéd?

questions.title: Should a payment ledger use event sourcing?
questions.prompt: A team proposes event sourcing for a payment ledger because audit history is important. Evaluate the decision.
questions.answerStructureHint: Establish whether history is the source of business truth, then cover concurrency, projections, evolution, privacy, and alternatives.

expectedPoints.label: Tests whether event history has business value
expectedPoints.description: Distinguishes a true need to reconstruct financial state from merely needing an audit log.

expectedPoints.label: Defines events and stream boundaries
expectedPoints.description: Uses immutable domain facts, aggregate streams, and optimistic concurrency.

expectedPoints.label: Plans read models and reconciliation
expectedPoints.description: Builds projections for queries and explains how they are checked, replayed, and repaired.

expectedPoints.label: Owns event evolution and compliance
expectedPoints.description: Covers event versioning, upcasting, retention, encryption, and deletion obligations.

expectedPoints.label: Compares simpler alternatives
expectedPoints.description: Considers a conventional ledger with immutable entries and audit log if full event sourcing adds no value.

followUps.prompt: How would you correct a historical mistake if old events must remain immutable?

hu.title: Kell-e event sourcing egy payment ledgerhez?
hu.prompt: A team event sourcingot javasol egy payment ledgerhez, mert fontos az audit history. Értékeld a döntést.
hu.answerStructureHint: Vizsgáld meg, hogy a history maga business source of truth-e, majd térj ki concurrencyre, projectionre, evolutionre, privacyra és alternatívára.

arch_es_value.label: Megvizsgálja a history business értékét
arch_es_value.description: Különbséget tesz a financial state rekonstruálása és egy egyszerű audit log igénye között.

arch_es_model.label: Definiálja az eventet és stream boundaryt
arch_es_model.description: Immutable domain factet, aggregate streamet és optimistic concurrencyt használ.

arch_es_reads.label: Read modelt és reconciliationt tervez
arch_es_reads.description: Projectiont épít, és elmagyarázza annak check, replay és repair módját.

arch_es_evolution.label: Vállalja az event evolutiont és compliance-t
arch_es_evolution.description: Event versioning, upcasting, retention, encryption és deletion obligation szerepel.

arch_es_cost.label: Egyszerűbb alternatívát is összehasonlít
arch_es_cost.description: Append-only ledger és audit log is felmerül, ha a full event sourcing nem ad elég értéket.

followUps.arch_es_followup_correction: Hogyan javítanál egy historical mistake-et, ha a régi eventek immutable-ek maradnak?

questions.title: Writing an architecture decision record
questions.prompt: Your team must choose between a managed message broker and operating one itself. What should the ADR contain?
questions.answerStructureHint: Capture context and constraints, compare options against scenarios, record consequences, ownership, migration, and review triggers.

expectedPoints.label: States context and decision scope
expectedPoints.description: Explains the workloads, current pain, constraints, assumptions, and what the decision does not cover.

expectedPoints.label: Compares realistic options
expectedPoints.description: Includes managed, self-operated, and possibly no-change options using the same criteria.

expectedPoints.label: Evaluates quality and operational trade-offs
expectedPoints.description: Covers availability, throughput, latency, security, compliance, skills, support, lock-in, and cost.

expectedPoints.label: Records consequences and risks
expectedPoints.description: States accepted limitations, failure modes, migration steps, rollback, and observability.

expectedPoints.label: Defines ownership and review
expectedPoints.description: Records owner, status, date, decision trigger, and conditions that would cause reconsideration.

followUps.prompt: How would you avoid comparing only the infrastructure invoice and missing the operational cost?

hu.title: Architecture Decision Record írása
hu.prompt: A teamnek managed message broker és saját üzemeltetés között kell választania. Mit tartalmazzon az ADR?
hu.answerStructureHint: Rögzítsd a contextet és constraintet, ugyanazon scenario-k alapján hasonlítsd az opciókat, majd írd le a consequence-et, ownershipöt, migrationt és review triggert.

arch_adr_context.label: Leírja a contextet és decision scope-ot
arch_adr_context.description: Workload, current pain, constraint, assumption és out-of-scope rész szerepel.

arch_adr_options.label: Reális opciókat hasonlít össze
arch_adr_options.description: Managed, self-operated és szükség esetén no-change opció ugyanazon criteria alapján jelenik meg.

arch_adr_attributes.label: Quality és operational trade-offot értékel
arch_adr_attributes.description: Availability, throughput, latency, security, compliance, skill, support, lock-in és cost szerepel.

arch_adr_consequences.label: Rögzíti a consequence-et és risket
arch_adr_consequences.description: Accepted limitation, failure mode, migration, rollback és observability kerül a dokumentumba.

arch_adr_owner.label: Ownershipöt és review-t definiál
arch_adr_owner.description: Owner, status, date és reconsideration condition szerepel.

followUps.arch_adr_followup_cost: Hogyan kerülnéd el, hogy csak az infrastructure invoice-t hasonlítsd össze, az operational costot pedig kihagyd?

questions.title: Architecture and team structure
questions.prompt: A central integration team must approve and deploy every change across twelve domain teams. What architecture and organisational risks do you see?
questions.answerStructureHint: Connect the approval queue to Conway’s Law, identify false service autonomy, and propose ownership plus platform changes.

expectedPoints.label: Identifies the coordination bottleneck
expectedPoints.description: Explains that one approval and deployment queue limits delivery across all domains.

expectedPoints.label: Connects it to Conway’s Law
expectedPoints.description: Shows how the central team encourages a centralised integration architecture and shared critical path.

expectedPoints.label: Recognizes false autonomy
expectedPoints.description: Notes that separately deployed services are not autonomous if one team controls their integrations and releases.

expectedPoints.label: Proposes end-to-end ownership
expectedPoints.description: Moves domain-specific logic and operations toward stream-aligned teams with explicit contracts.

expectedPoints.label: Reframes the platform team
expectedPoints.description: Keeps reusable self-service tooling, standards, and guardrails without becoming a ticket gate.

followUps.prompt: How would you preserve security and integration standards without restoring the same central approval bottleneck?

hu.title: Architecture és team structure
hu.prompt: Egy central integration teamnek kell approve-olnia és deployolnia minden change-et tizenkét domain team számára. Milyen architecture és organisational risket látsz?
hu.answerStructureHint: Kösd az approval queue-t Conway’s Law-hoz, azonosítsd a false service autonomy-t, majd javasolj ownership és platform változást.

arch_conway_bottleneck.label: Felismeri a coordination bottlenecket
arch_conway_bottleneck.description: Egyetlen approval és deployment queue korlátozza minden domain deliveryjét.

arch_conway_law.label: Conway’s Law-hoz köti
arch_conway_law.description: A central team centralised integration architecture-t és shared critical pathot erősít.

arch_conway_autonomy.label: Felismeri a false autonomy-t
arch_conway_autonomy.description: A külön deployolt service sem autonomous, ha minden integrationt és release-t egy team irányít.

arch_conway_change.label: End-to-end ownershipöt javasol
arch_conway_change.description: Domain-specific logic és operation stream-aligned teamhez kerül explicit contracttal.

arch_conway_platform.label: Újradefiniálja a platform teamet
arch_conway_platform.description: Reusable self-service toolingot, standardot és guardrailt ad ticket gate nélkül.

followUps.arch_conway_followup_governance: Hogyan tartanád meg a security és integration standardokat anélkül, hogy visszaépítenéd ugyanazt a central approval bottlenecket?
